settings.heading: Why bag-in-box (BIB)?
settings.row_content: It offers fantastic value for the money. It’s also “no sweat”, it’s lightweight, totable and once opened, it protects the wine from oxidation for an extended period of time. It’s also a super convenient to have a single glass or two whenever you want it. And it’s more eco-friendly than traditional glass bottles with a smaller carbon footprint. The box is recyclable. Each box is equal to four standard (750ml) bottles of wine.

settings.heading: Why is Joi sourced from Chile?
settings.row_content: We’ve searched far and wide to find the right partner. In terms of value for the money, Chile is a “far out” source with a long history of providing excellent price/quality for the money. We also wanted to partner with a winery that makes wines with a true sense of place, along with having the best technical ability for a great quality bag-in-box wine. In addition, Joi is produced as a Certified Sustainable Wine of Chile, Certified BRC, Certified IFS, Certified Global Gap and its vegan friendly.

settings.heading: What occasions are right for drinking Joi?
settings.row_content: “You can dig it” for casual wine drinking with friends or when you just need a single glass or two?

settings.heading: What is the wine style of Joi?
settings.row_content: Both the Chardonnay and Cabernet Sauvignon are made to allow the pure, primary fruit character to shine through. The Chardonnay is fresh, crisp, bright, and lively with a minimal influence of oak. The Cabernet Sauvignon is deep, full of nicely concentrated flavors and minimal tannin to better allow for a soft finish. Unlike many other widely available box wines, Joi is very low in residual sugar.

settings.heading: What is the best way to store Joi?
settings.row_content: Temperature extremes are no friends of wine. Store the wines at room temperature, away from direct sunlight. Don’t leave it in the trunk of your car or in a snowbank, which would be a “bummer”!

settings.heading: What is the best temperature for serving Joi?
settings.row_content: Serve the Chardonnay at refrigerator temperature. Pop it in the fridge for at least two hours before serving. The Cabernet Sauvignon is best served at room temperature or slightly chilled.

settings.heading: What is the shelf life of Joi?
settings.row_content: Unopened, the wines can keep a full two years from the vintage date.

settings.heading: How long can Joi keep opened?
settings.row_content: Once the package is opened, the wine will keep for up to six weeks. Storing the opened container in the fridge will help extend the life as well.

settings.heading: How many calories are in a glass of Joi?
settings.row_content: A four-ounce pour has about one hundred calories.
